content.problemStatement:
Create a program to perform the following:


Declare two integer variables **a** and **b**.
Assign the value 202020 to **a** and 666 to **b**.
print the result of **a / b** displaying only the integer part of the division, without the decimal portion.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000), the number of test cases. No further input is required for each test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the integer part of 202020 divided by 666.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case. 202020 ÷ 666 = 303.303... The integer part is 303.
Two test cases, each produces the same integer division result 303.
Five identical outputs because the computation does not depend on any per‑test input.

solution.approach: The values of a and b are fixed (a = 202020, b = 666). For each test case we compute a / b using integer division, which discards the fractional part. The result is the same for every test case, so we simply repeat the pre‑computed value.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time division per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    T = int(data[0])
    a = 202020
    b = 666
    result = a // b  # integer division
    out = (str(result) + '\n') * T
    sys.stdout.write(out)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
